macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: High Oil Prices Trigger Stock Market Selloff and Dash Fed Rate Cut Hopes
items.summary: Oil prices surged with Brent crude up 3.3% to $112.19 per barrel and U.S. crude up 2.3% to $98.32, causing major indices to decline: S&P 500 fell 1.5% to 6,506.48, Dow dropped 1% or 443 points to 45,577.47, and Nasdaq tumbled 2% to 21,647.61. Traders abandoned bets on Federal Reserve rate cuts this year amid fears of potential hikes in 2026 due to persistent high energy costs.

items.title: Super Micro Computer Stock Plunges 33% on U.S. Smuggling Charges
items.summary: Super Micro Computer shares lost a third of their value, falling 33.3%, after the U.S. government accused a senior VP and affiliates of conspiring to smuggle billions in servers with advanced Nvidia chips to China, weighing heavily on the broader market.

items.title: FedEx Shares Rise on Strong Quarterly Profit Beat
items.summary: FedEx stock increased 0.8% after reporting much stronger-than-expected profits for its latest quarter, standing out as one of the few gainers amid Friday's market downturn.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $69K Amid Quadruple Witching Event
items.summary: Bitcoin is trading around $69,733 as a record $7.1 trillion in derivatives expire today in the largest quadruple witching event ever recorded. The crypto market shows mixed sentiment with a Fear and Greed Index at 30, while Ethereum trades at $2,127.

items.title: AI Tokens Lead Market Gains as Altcoins Rally
items.summary: AI and payment sector tokens are outperforming, with KITE surging 10.1%, FET rising 7.3%, and PI Network gaining 5.3% ahead of its Protocol v21 mainnet upgrade. Broader sentiment remains cautious amid ETF outflows.

items.title: Bitcoin Outperforms Traditional Assets Amid Global Volatility
items.summary: Bitcoin has shown remarkable relative strength with only a 4.5% retraction compared to gold's 10% decline and significant S&P 500 losses. Long-term metrics suggest Bitcoin remains in a historically low-risk accumulation zone despite short-term sideways action.

items.title: Whale Accumulation Signals Renewed Interest in Bitcoin
items.summary: A whale withdrew 460 BTC worth $32.41 million from Binance after three months of inactivity, indicating renewed large-scale accumulation activity despite macro uncertainty.

items.title: Coinbase CEO Frames Bitcoin as Fiscal Discipline Tool
items.summary: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong positioned Bitcoin as a check on inflation and fiscal excess, arguing that capital moves to Bitcoin when government spending becomes excessive. This reflects a shift in how industry leaders position crypto's role in the financial system.

items.title: Quant (QNT) Trending After Robinhood Listing
items.summary: Quant (QNT) became the #1 trending token on March 19 following its Robinhood listing. Analysts caution that tokens reaching the top of trending lists often face short-term corrections as hype subsides.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Cerebras Partners with AWS to Boost AI Inference Speed
items.summary: AWS is deploying Cerebras CS-3 systems via AWS Bedrock to offer faster AI inference using open-source LLMs and Amazon's Nova models. The disaggregated architecture pairs AWS Trainium for prefill with Cerebras WSE for decode, achieving 5x boost in token throughput.

items.title: Moonshot AI Introduces Attention Residuals for Transformer Layers
items.summary: Moonshot AI presents Attention Residuals (AttnRes), a method allowing transformer layers to reference earlier layers instead of simply adding outputs. This architectural innovation improves how deep neural networks combine information across layers.

items.title: AI2 Releases Olmo Hybrid with 2x Data Efficiency
items.summary: AI2 released Olmo Hybrid, a 7B-parameter open model combining transformer attention with linear recurrent layers. The model achieves the same MMLU accuracy as Olmo 3 using 49% fewer tokens, demonstrating significant improvements in data efficiency.

items.title: Google Introduces Bayesian Teaching Method for LLMs
items.summary: Google researchers developed Bayesian teaching, a training method that teaches large language models to update beliefs as new information appears. The approach enables models to maintain uncertainty, weigh evidence, and improve predictions over time.

items.title: Deep Learning Model Advances Nuclear Charge Density Prediction
items.summary: Jilin University researchers developed a physics-informed deep neural network that predicts nuclear charge density distributions with high precision. The model integrates physical mechanisms with machine learning using a two-stage optimization process.

items.title: University of Phoenix Presents Human-AI Collaboration Research
items.summary: University of Phoenix researchers presented studies on human-AI cognitive collaboration and AI-ready healthcare curriculum at the AECT 2026 conference. The research includes frameworks for distributing cognitive tasks between humans and AI systems.

items.title: DigitalXForce Advances AI Governance and Enterprise Risk Management
items.summary: DigitalXForce is promoting a holistic approach to enterprise risk management integrating cybersecurity, compliance, and AI governance. The company emphasizes that digital trust will be the defining currency of the AI economy.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Launches with Poor Console Performance
items.summary: Crimson Desert, an action-adventure game by Pearl Abyss, released on March 19, 2026, for PS5, Xbox Series X/S, Windows, and macOS, but console versions suffer from stuttering frame rates and major performance issues, leading to criticism that it feels unoptimized.[3]

items.title: Highguard Hero-Shooter Shuts Down After Less Than 50 Days
items.summary: Highguard, a hero-shooter, shut down on March 12, 2026, just short of 50 days after launch, becoming an internet meme and highlighting the gaming industry's ruthless nature toward underperforming titles.[2]

items.title: Ubisoft Cancels Six Games and Closes Studios
items.summary: Ubisoft announced the cancellation of six unrevealed video games and closure of studios in Sweden and Canada earlier this year, including the Prince of Persia: Sands of Time remake.[2]

items.title: VideoGamer Site Delisted from Google After AI Content Pivot
items.summary: Legacy games media site VideoGamer was removed from Google search results following its pivot to AI-generated articles, as discussed in recent gaming news roundup.[1]

items.title: Nvidia Plans Open-Source AI Agent Platform Ahead of GTC
items.summary: Nvidia is reportedly launching an open-source AI agent platform, inspired by projects like OpenClaw, with pre-GTC news leaking on March 20, 2026.[1]

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Urges iPhone Users to Update Software Amid Mass Hacking Campaigns
items.summary: Apple warns iPhone users to immediately update their software to protect against widespread hacking threats targeting older iOS versions. The company also launched AirPods Max 2, with rumors of a major redesign next year.

items.title: Samsung Discontinues Galaxy Z Trifold Due to Memory Issues
items.summary: Samsung has discontinued its Galaxy Z trifold smartphone after just months, citing memory crunch issues despite its success as a three-screen proof of concept.

items.title: Samsung Confirms Galaxy S26 to Gain AirDrop Support via Quick Share
items.summary: Samsung confirmed at a Japan press event that the Galaxy S26 series will receive AirDrop-like functionality through Google's Quick Share via upcoming software updates.

items.title: Elve Achieves First E-Band Wireless Transmission Using TWTA for Cellular Backhaul
items.summary: Elve demonstrated the first full-duplex, bi-directional E-band wireless radio transmission using its Vermillion TWTA technology with Nokia radios, delivering over 10 times the power of existing mmWave backhaul radios at 10 Gbps.

items.title: OnePlus Watch 4 Leak Reveals Two Variants and Wear OS 6
items.summary: Leaked details indicate the OnePlus Watch 4 is nearing launch with two variants running Wear OS 6.

items.title: Private Space Company Unveils Plan to Capture Asteroid After ISS Tech Tests
items.summary: A private space company announced a bold plan to capture an asteroid, following successful technology tests aboard the International Space Station.
